title: pouteria kerrii
intro: Pouteria kerrii, also known as the Thai Chestnut or Kerri Pouteria, is a tropical tree native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its large, edible fruit and dense, evergreen foliage. The medicinal properties of Pouteria kerrii lie in its seeds, which contain a compound called lupeol, a triterpenoid with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of consuming Pouteria kerrii seeds include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an antioxidant, while also supporting the immune system. Pouteria kerrii is often prepared as a herbal tea or decoction, and its seeds can also be ground into a fine powder for use in traditional medicine.
